files.path: I’m a product leader and want to know what to buil.md
files.reason: Architectural spine of the playbook. Tier-1 specifics on what to build first (catalog API, intent-to-cart, agent checkout, post-purchase). Tier-1 roadmap table with time horizons. Tier-2 canonical 5-primitive stack. Tier-1 agent-readiness scorecard.

files.path: Steps to launch a SaaS tool monetized by agent mic.md
files.reason: Operational 10-step playbook for a distinct monetisation lane (agent micropayments SaaS — adjacent to but separate from shopping agents). No overlap with other files. Folds directly into the 'Agent micropayments SaaS' section.

files.path: What data and systems do retail AI agents need to.md
files.reason: Cleanest articulation of the minimum data stack in the corpus. The 7-item practical-minimum-stack checklist is the most actionable content from this file. Reinforces the 5-primitive stack from file 3.

files.path: “We’re still going to be influenced by the people.md
files.reason: The 10 ranked Claude-SDK wedge ideas with core idea / leverage / SDK-fit per idea are the most concrete, actionable content in the entire corpus. Folded into 'Build wedges' tier ranking. The Tobi Lütke quote and closing Q&A prompts are discarded.

files.path: _archive/Alibaba (9988.HK) is preparing to unveil the integ.md
files.reason: Single-vendor news article (Qwen + Taobao). Specific facts (4B SKUs, virtual try-on, 30-day price tracking) are vendor-specific and not actionable for a builder. The two valuable framings — China-vs-West integrated-stack vs protocol-play, and the 'agent-for-human vs agent-for-agent' gap — are captured in the playbook's Strategic Frames section.

files.path: _archive/Examples of AI personal shoppers like OpenAI Opera.md
files.reason: Short overview file. The 5 Claude SDK product ideas it lists are fully subsumed by the 10 ranked ideas in the larger 'We're still going to be influenced' file. The wedge-selection criteria (clear intent + repeat + low risk) are captured in the build-wedges section.

files.path: _archive/What are top use cases for AI agents in retail 202.md
files.reason: Generic 'top use cases' overview. Every use case named (personal shopping, customer support, inventory forecasting, merchandising, agentic checkout) is stated more concretely or more usefully elsewhere. No tier-1/2/3 content for someone with marketing expertise.

files.path: _archive/What does this mean for agent-agent commerce in 20.md
files.reason: Mostly futurism (2026–2030 speculation). The genuinely useful framings — power shifts to back-end execution, pricing pressure from buyer agents, rendezvous-point concentration — are 1–2 sentences each and fold cleanly into the playbook's Strategic Frames. The bulk of the file is repetition of consensus AI-commerce predictions.
